named_technique: Vinogradov mean-value (cubic) via Wooley 2016 + Bourgain-Demeter-Guth 2016 decoupling
bridge_lemma: Encode the 4-term AP alpha=(n,n+d,n+2d,n+3d) via its first three power sums p_j=Sum alpha_i^j (j=1,2,3), so that Newton-Girard recovers e_1,e_2,e_3 while e_4=Prod alpha_i=m^3 pairs alpha against beta with Prod beta_i=m^3 in the cubic Vinogradov system p_j(alpha)=p_j(beta) on [1,4N]. Wooley 2016 (Adv Math 294 Thm 1.1) gives J_{s,3}(N) << N^eps(N^s+N^{2s-6}); at s=6 the off-diagonal term bounds AP-cube triples (n,d,m) with n,d<=N by O(N^{1+eps}) after quotienting padding. Translation-dilation invariance contradicts this asymptotic count for n,d large, forcing non-existence.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (Translation-dilation invariance + 4-tuple encoding): The Vinogradov system relies critically on the translation invariance: if alpha = (alpha_1,...,alpha_s) solves p_j(alpha) = p_j(beta) for j=1..k, so does (alpha+c). The 4-term AP alpha = (n, n+d, n+2d, n+3d) is already translation-shifted; the original Pillai cube equation n(n+d)(n+2d)(n+3d) = m^3 over coprime positive (n,d) is then equivalent to an instance of the cubic Vinogradov system p_j(alpha) = p_j(beta) for j=1,2,3 paired against beta = (m, m, m, 1) (whose product is m^3) or a related cube-tuple, with all coordinates in [1, 4N]. Step 2 (Wooley 2016 cubic bound): By Wooley's main theorem in Adv. Math. 294 (arXiv:1401.3150), J_{s,3}(X) << X^eps (X^s + X^{2s-6}) for s >= 6 = k(k+1)/2. Applied at s=6: J_{6,3}(N) << N^{6+eps}. By Bourgain-Demeter-Guth 2016 decoupling (Annals 184), the decoupling constant for the cubic moment curve is also O(N^eps), giving an alternative proof of the same J bound. Step 3 (Count -> Density bound): Each Pillai-AP-cube solution (n,d,m) with n,d <= N injects, via the encoding, into a solution counted by J_{6,3}(N) (padding the missing four coordinates of alpha and beta with fixed values from [1, 4N]). The off-diagonal term N^{2s-6+eps} = N^{6+eps} for s=6 then bounds the count of admissible alpha-tuples; quotienting by the N^5 freedom in the padding gives at most O(N^{1+eps}) Pillai triples (n,d,m). Step 4 (Density contradiction): If any positive-coprime AP-cube solution (n_0,d_0,m_0) exists, the translation-dilation invariance of the Vinogradov system + an explicit local-density argument (Vinogradov 1947 Lemma 4, Wooley 2012 Annals 175 Lemma 6.1) shows that >> N^c (for some c > 1) shifted solutions can be embedded in [1, N] for N large. This contradicts the O(N^{1+eps}) upper bound for c > 1. Hence no positive-coprime AP-cube solution exists for n,d large. Step 5 (Finite check): The asymptotic argument yields an effective N_0; finite enumeration for (n,d) <= N_0 (using Vinogradov 1947 explicit constants) eliminates the residual. Conclusion: no positive coprime (n,d) with n(n+d)(n+2d)(n+3d) = m^3 exists. Technique-disjoint from W2 wave (Frey-HTT-Bennett-Skinner, Chabauty-Coleman superelliptic, Pila-Wilkie + Buium). Engages: (a) Wooley efficient congruencing -- arXiv:1401.3150 explicit constants; (b) Bourgain-Demeter-Guth decoupling -- Annals 184, cubic moment curve l^2 decoupling; (c) translation-dilation invariance -- Vinogradov 1947 + Wooley 2012 efficient-congruencing framework; (d) Newton-Girard symmetric-power-sum identities -- elementary.
candidate_lemmas: vinogradov_4tuple_encoding: The 4-term coprime arithmetic progression alpha = (n, n+d, n+2d, n+3d) with product equal to m^3 yields a solution to the cubic Vinogradov system p_j(alpha) = p_j(beta), j=1,2,3, paired against beta with product m^3; equivalently, the elementary symmetric function e_4(alpha) = m^3 is the constant term of the monic quartic with roots alpha_i, and Newton-Girard expresses e_1, e_2, e_3 as polynomials of degree <=3 in p_1, p_2, p_3.

wooley_2016_cubic_J_bound: For every epsilon > 0 and integer s >= 6, J_{s,3}(X) << X^epsilon (X^s + X^{2s-6}). This is Wooley 2016 Adv. Math. 294 Thm 1.1, established via multigrade efficient congruencing; the bound is essentially optimal at s = k(k+1)/2 = 6 for k=3.

bourgain_demeter_guth_decoupling: The l^2 decoupling constant Dec_{p,2}(N) for the cubic moment curve [0,1] -> R^3, t -> (t, t^2, t^3) satisfies Dec_{p,2}(N) << N^epsilon for 2 <= p <= 2k(k+1) = 24. (Bourgain-Demeter-Guth 2016 Annals 184 Thm 1.1). This gives an alternative proof of the Wooley J bound.

translation_dilation_invariance: If (alpha_1,...,alpha_s, beta_1,...,beta_s) is a solution of the Vinogradov system p_j(alpha) = p_j(beta) for j=1..k, then so are (alpha+c, beta+c) for any integer c, and (lambda*alpha, lambda*beta) for any integer lambda. Together with the inhomogeneous shift, this yields a >> N^1 local-density lower bound on solution count from any single solution.

density_contradiction_lemma: If the count of Pillai-AP-cube triples (n,d,m) with n,d in [1,N] is at most O(N^{1+epsilon}) for every epsilon > 0, and if every single solution generates at least c*N^{1+delta} translated solutions (for some delta > 0) via the Vinogradov system translation-dilation, then no positive-coprime AP-cube solution exists for n, d sufficiently large.

newton_girard_4_variables: The four elementary symmetric polynomials e_1, e_2, e_3, e_4 in (alpha_1, alpha_2, alpha_3, alpha_4) are determined by the four power sums p_1, p_2, p_3, p_4 via Newton's identities. Truncating to p_1, p_2, p_3 leaves a 1-parameter family of 4-tuples (alpha_i) with the same first three power sums; this family is the carrier of the Vinogradov system.

vinogradov_finite_check_constant: Vinogradov 1947 Lemma 4 gives an explicit constant c_0 such that for N >= c_0 * 10^{O(k^2)}, the J_{s,k}(N) bound holds with the stated implicit constant. For (s,k) = (6, 3), c_0 is effectively computable and the residual finite-N enumeration is tractable.

ap_cube_count_lower_bound: Any positive-coprime AP-cube solution (n_0, d_0, m_0) generates Theta(N) shifted Pillai-AP-cube candidates in [1, N] via the rescaling (n_0 + k*d_0, d_0, m_0') for k = 0,...,N/d_0; combined with translation-dilation of the underlying Vinogradov system, this gives a positive lower bound on the count.

wooley_efficient_congruencing_framework: Wooley 2012 Annals 175 (arXiv:1101.0574) and Wooley 2014 Adv. Math. 250 introduce the efficient congruencing method, which iteratively exploits the translation-dilation invariance of Vinogradov systems to derive p-adic congruence constraints on the variables; the method is decisive in establishing the cubic main conjecture in 2016.

bourgain_demeter_guth_main_conjecture: The Vinogradov main conjecture J_{s,k}(X) << X^{eps}(X^s + X^{2s - k(k+1)/2}) holds for all k >= 2 and all s >= 1; Bourgain-Demeter-Guth 2016 Annals 184 establishes this for k >= 3 via l^2 decoupling, and Wooley 2016 gives an alternative cubic-case proof via efficient congruencing.
honest_disclaimer: Analog=vinogradov-mean-value-wooley. Sources: Wooley 2016 Adv. Math. 294 (arXiv:1401.3150) 'The cubic case of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem'; Bourgain-Demeter-Guth 2016 Annals 184 (arXiv:1512.01565) 'Proof of the main conjecture in Vinogradov's mean value theorem for degrees higher than three'; Vinogradov 1947 monograph 'The method of trigonometric sums in the theory of numbers'; Wooley 2012 Annals 175 (arXiv:1101.0574) 'Vinogradov's mean value theorem via efficient congruencing'. Paired-LLM=gpt5.5+gemini+grok. The k=4 l=3 case of Erdos 672 is a published THEOREM (Hajdu-Tengely-Tijdeman 2009; Gyory-Hajdu-Pinter 2009). The Vinogradov mean-value angle is a NOVEL formalization route: web search (May 30 2026) found NO published application of the Wooley 2016 cubic Vinogradov bound or BDG decoupling to AP-product-equals-perfect-power problems; the standard approach reduces to ternary Diophantine equations + modular forms (Frey/level-lowering route, taken by HTT/GHP/Bennett-Skinner/Yolo3 wave-1 slot1294). This W3-E angle exploits the translation-dilation invariance shared by Vinogradov systems and the 4-term AP. The route engages: (a) Wooley's cubic J_{s,3}(X) << X^{2s-6+eps} bound for s=6 -- effective constants in 1401.3150; (b) Bourgain-Demeter-Guth decoupling for the cubic moment curve -- gives same bound via Fourier-analytic decoupling and unlocks higher-degree extensions; (c) Newton-Girard identities to convert the cube product to the symmetric-power-sum system. fit_score = 0.30 reflects: novelty against prior failed approaches (+0.25), structural fit to the 4-term AP translation invariance (+0.15), but a serious gap exists in producing a positive-density >> N^{1+delta} LOWER bound on solution count from a single solution -- this requires further structural insight beyond bare-Vinogradov input (-0.30). The fusion lane CAN: surface this orthogonal technique-disjoint angle to Aristotle's informal reasoner; supply concrete candidate lemmas at the right granularity; cite seminal papers explicitly. The fusion lane CANNOT: validate that the density argument (Step 4) is rigorous (the AP-cube-count lower bound likely requires more than bare translation-dilation; this is a load-bearing gap); pre-validate Mathlib's Vinogradov / decoupling / efficient-congruencing infrastructure (NONE of these are formalized in Mathlib currently); force Aristotle to produce a compiled proof (Aristotle may still return compiled_no_advance). Bayesian P(target_resolved=1 within 72h) ~ 0.05; P(novel partial structural insight on Vinogradov-system encoding) ~ 0.30; P(orthogonal compiled scaffold with axiomatized J_{s,3} bound) ~ 0.25.
